bid_claims.quote_text: Each of Hudson’s Bay and Sponsor A indicated that they were considering making an offer to acquire Saks for at least $15 per share, in cash.

Saks received a letter from Company H, a privately held company based in the U.S. unknown to Saks and its advisors, purporting to propose to acquire Saks for an aggregate price of $2.6 billion in cash, with no details or further information.

event_claims.description: Sponsor A made an unsolicited initial acquisition contact to Saks' CEO.
event_claims.quote_text: In February 2013, Stephen I. Sadove, Saks’ Chairman and Chief Executive Officer, received an unsolicited phone call from a representative of a private equity firm, which we refer to as Sponsor A, expressing interest in a potential acquisition of Saks.

event_claims.description: Richard Baker of Hudson's Bay met with Saks' CEO to discuss a potential acquisition of Saks by Hudson's Bay.
event_claims.quote_text: On April 1, 2013, Mr. Sadove met with Richard Baker, the Director, Governor, and Chief Executive Officer of Hudson’s Bay, at the request of Mr. Baker and discussed a potential acquisition of Saks by Hudson’s Bay.

event_claims.description: The board directed management to continue exploring potential transactions.
event_claims.quote_text: The board directed Mr. Sadove and Saks management to continue exploring these potential transactions.

event_claims.description: The board authorized implementation of a process to determine whether a transaction with a potential acquiror that had executed a confidentiality agreement could be reached.
event_claims.quote_text: authorized (1) the implementation of a process to determine whether a transaction with one of the potential acquirors which had executed a confidentiality agreement could be reached on terms that would offer attractive value to Saks shareholders and (2) the submission of a revised proposal to acquire Company B.

event_claims.description: Sponsor A and Sponsor E were preliminarily prepared to submit an indicative proposal to acquire Saks.
event_claims.quote_text: Hudson’s Bay, on the one hand, and Sponsor A and Sponsor E, on the other hand, were each preliminarily prepared to proceed with the submission of a proposal to acquire Saks at indicative price ranges of $15 to $15.25 per share of common stock (in the case of Hudson’s Bay) and $15 to $16 per share of common stock (in the case of Sponsor A and Sponsor E).

event_claims.description: Hudson's Bay was preliminarily prepared to submit an indicative proposal to acquire Saks.
event_claims.quote_text: Hudson’s Bay, on the one hand, and Sponsor A and Sponsor E, on the other hand, were each preliminarily prepared to proceed with the submission of a proposal to acquire Saks at indicative price ranges of $15 to $15.25 per share of common stock (in the case of Hudson’s Bay) and $15 to $16 per share of common stock (in the case of Sponsor A and Sponsor E).

event_claims.description: Hudson's Bay submitted a first-round proposal expressing continued interest in acquiring Saks.
event_claims.quote_text: On July 11, 2013, each of Hudson’s Bay, on the one hand, and Sponsor E, together with Sponsor G, on the other hand, submitted proposals expressing their continued interest in an acquisition of Saks.

event_claims.description: Sponsor E and Sponsor G submitted a first-round joint proposal expressing continued interest in acquiring Saks.
event_claims.quote_text: On July 11, 2013, each of Hudson’s Bay, on the one hand, and Sponsor E, together with Sponsor G, on the other hand, submitted proposals expressing their continued interest in an acquisition of Saks.

event_claims.description: Sponsor G ceased participating in the process.
event_claims.quote_text: Saks was subsequently informed that Sponsor G was no longer participating in the process

event_claims.description: Hudson's Bay advised Goldman Sachs it was prepared to offer $16 per share and accept substantially all significant agreement and financing issues.
event_claims.quote_text: On July 24, 2013, representatives of Hudson’s Bay advised Goldman Sachs that, subject to negotiating a definitive, binding agreement, Hudson’s Bay was prepared to offer $16 per share of common stock and was agreeable to Saks’ position on substantially all of the significant issues in the draft merger agreement and the draft agreements relating to Hudson’s Bay’s committed financing of the merger conveyed by Goldman Sachs to Hudson’s Bay.

event_claims.description: The board authorized management and advisors to proceed with negotiations with Hudson's Bay to determine if an agreement could be reached.
event_claims.quote_text: The board authorized senior management and Saks’ advisors to proceed with negotiations with Hudson’s Bay to determine if an agreement could be reached.

event_claims.description: Hudson's Bay provided committed debt and equity financing information for the potential transaction.
event_claims.quote_text: Hudson’s Bay’s proposal included a price of $15.25 per share of common stock, a revised draft merger agreement and information and documentation relating to Hudson’s Bay’s committed debt and equity financing for the potential transaction.

event_claims.actor_label: Saks, Hudson’s Bay and Merger Sub
event_claims.description: Saks, Hudson's Bay and Merger Sub executed the merger agreement and other transaction documents.
event_claims.quote_text: Following the board’s approval of the merger and the merger agreement, Saks, Hudson’s Bay and Merger Sub finalized and executed the merger agreement and other transaction documents later on July 28, 2013.

participation_count_claims.quote_text: Hudson’s Bay, on the one hand, and Sponsor A and Sponsor E, on the other hand, were each preliminarily prepared to proceed with the submission of a proposal to acquire Saks at indicative price ranges of $15 to $15.25 per share of common stock (in the case of Hudson’s Bay) and $15 to $16 per share of common stock (in the case of Sponsor A and Sponsor E).
On July 11, 2013, each of Hudson’s Bay, on the one hand, and Sponsor E, together with Sponsor G, on the other hand, submitted proposals expressing their continued interest in an acquisition of Saks.
